findings.description: State machine contains suspiciously named state 'T_ARMED' (matched keyword: 'armed'). Hidden or unreachable states are a common trojan insertion technique.
findings.recommendation: Review the state machine for unreachable states and verify all transitions are intentional and documented.

findings.line_content: localparam T_DEAD  = 3'd4;   // Unreachable in normal operation
findings.description: State machine contains suspiciously named state 'T_DEAD' (matched keyword: 'dead'). Hidden or unreachable states are a common trojan insertion technique.
findings.recommendation: Review the state machine for unreachable states and verify all transitions are intentional and documented.

findings.description: Register with suspicious name detected. Flags named 'armed', 'trojan_active', 'kill_switch', or 'exfil_mode' suggest trojan activation logic.
findings.recommendation: Review the purpose of this control flag and its drivers.

findings.description: Register with suspicious name detected. Flags named 'armed', 'trojan_active', 'kill_switch', or 'exfil_mode' suggest trojan activation logic.
findings.recommendation: Review the purpose of this control flag and its drivers.

findings.description: Output 'carry_out' is conditionally modified based on suspicious signal (matched: 'trojan'). This could indicate unauthorized manipulation of design outputs.
findings.recommendation: Verify that all paths modifying 'carry_out' are part of the intended specification.

findings.description: Output 'zero_flag' is conditionally modified based on suspicious signal (matched: 'trojan'). This could indicate unauthorized manipulation of design outputs.
findings.recommendation: Verify that all paths modifying 'zero_flag' are part of the intended specification.

findings.description: Signal 'trigger_cnt' compared against 32-bit constant 0xDEAD_BEEF. Large counter comparisons are a classic hardware trojan time-bomb activation pattern.
findings.recommendation: Verify that the comparison value 0xDEAD_BEEF has a legitimate design purpose. Document the expected activation count if intentional.

findings.description: Dynamic bit-indexing using counter-derived index. This pattern can be used to extract internal data bit-by-bit through a side channel.
findings.recommendation: Review whether dynamic bit selection is functionally required. Consider if this could leak sensitive data.

findings.description: Known magic constant 'DE (trigger pattern)' detected. These values are commonly used as trojan activation triggers.
findings.recommendation: Replace magic constants with named parameters and document their purpose.

findings.description: Known magic constant 'AD (trigger pattern)' detected. These values are commonly used as trojan activation triggers.
findings.recommendation: Replace magic constants with named parameters and document their purpose.

findings.description: Known magic constant 'DEAD_BEEF' detected. These values are commonly used as trojan activation triggers.
findings.recommendation: Replace magic constants with named parameters and document their purpose.

findings.line_content: reg        exfil_mode;           // HT-005: Covert channel flag
findings.description: Register with suspicious name detected. Flags named 'armed', 'trojan_active', 'kill_switch', or 'exfil_mode' suggest trojan activation logic.
findings.recommendation: Review the purpose of this control flag and its drivers.

findings.description: Output multiplexer with suspicious mode selector. Conditional output switching between normal and 'exfiltration' paths is a hallmark of covert channel trojans.
findings.recommendation: Remove the conditional output path and verify the signal drives only intended functionality.

findings.description: Known magic constant 'BA (trigger pattern)' detected. These values are commonly used as trojan activation triggers.
findings.recommendation: Replace magic constants with named parameters and document their purpose.
